name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BUG-2
title: dispatch-tdd-subagent.py only auto-closes the primary --linked-item; secondary bugs fixed by same commit remain open
description:
dispatch-tdd-subagent.py accepts only a single --linked-item / --item-type pair. On test-green it calls item-status.py once for that one item. When a TDD cycle fixes multiple bugs (e.g., BUG-8 and BUG-9 were both addressed by the same commit in PR #102), only the primary linked-item is auto-closed; secondary bugs fixed by the same commit remain open and must be closed manually.

Observed: PR #102 fixed both RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-8 (primary, --linked-item) and RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-9 (secondary, same root cause). BUG-9 had to be closed manually after the TDD cycle completed.

Fix: extend dispatch-tdd-subagent.py with a --linked-items flag (comma-separated list of <feature>:<type>:<id> triples) that supplements the existing --linked-item / --item-type pair. On test-green, call item-status.py for each entry in --linked-items with the same impl_commit and reason. The existing --linked-item / --item-type interface remains unchanged (backward-compatible). The HANDOFF block should list all closed items.

Format proposal for --linked-items:
  --linked-items "rabbit-cage:bug:RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-9,rabbit-file:bug:RABBIT-FILE-BUG-2"

Callers (rabbit-feature-touch Step 5) pass --linked-items when they know multiple items are resolved by the same cycle. The rabbit-file Work Protocol Step 5 is responsible for assembling this list before dispatching the TDD subagent.

history.note: TDD cycle complete. dispatch-tdd-subagent.py now accepts --linked-items (comma-separated <feature>:<type>:<id> triples) for secondary items resolved by the same cycle. HANDOFF lists all closed items via closed_items: field. 9 new e2e tests.
